state_description: Waiting for human approval of the plan
product_description: AI-powered personal tutor with adaptive learning paths, Socratic questioning, multi-subject support, progress analytics, and parent dashboard.

plan.description: Define business requirements, user personas (students K-12, parents, educators), user stories, learning outcome KPIs, and ROI model for ai_tutor platform
acceptance_criteria: At least 3 distinct user personas documented with goals and pain points
Minimum 30 user stories covering student, parent, and admin roles
ROI model shows unit economics for B2C and B2B2C pricing tiers
Learning outcome KPIs defined: mastery rate, session completion, time-to-mastery, 7-day retention

plan.description: Produce PRD covering adaptive learning paths, Socratic questioning engine, multi-subject support (Math, Science, Language Arts, History), progress analytics, and parent dashboard. Define MVP scope, versioned roadmap phases, and success metrics.
acceptance_criteria: PRD covers all 5 core features with acceptance criteria per feature
Roadmap has at least 3 versioned phases with release criteria
Success metrics include DAU, 7-day retention, mastery rate, and NPS
MVP scope document signed off with no scope-creep items in v1

plan.description: Analyze edtech competitor landscape (Khan Academy, Khanmigo, Synthesis, IXL, Duolingo), identify differentiation opportunities in AI-personalized Socratic tutoring, and define go-to-market positioning.
acceptance_criteria: Competitive matrix covers at least 6 competitors across 8 evaluation dimensions
Clear differentiation statement articulated in one sentence
GTM strategy defines two launch channels with CAC estimates
Pricing tiers proposed with competitive benchmarking justification

plan.description: Conduct user research with student and parent archetypes, produce information architecture, wireframes for all core flows (student onboarding, Socratic learning session, progress dashboard, parent review), and accessibility audit plan targeting WCAG 2.1 AA.
acceptance_criteria: IA diagram covers all routes with depth <= 3 clicks to any core feature
Wireframes cover student onboarding, Socratic session, quiz, progress, and parent dashboard flows
Accessibility checklist maps each UI element to WCAG 2.1 AA criteria
Age-appropriate UI patterns documented per age group with design rationale
Usability test plan defines at least 5 tasks with measurable success criteria
Accessibility for learners with disabilities
Mobile-responsive for student devices

plan.description: Produce COPPA compliance plan (verifiable parental consent, data minimization, parental access and deletion rights), FERPA compliance checklist (education records, school official exception, disclosure controls), and WCAG 2.1 conformance strategy.
acceptance_criteria: COPPA plan covers verifiable parental consent mechanism via email-plus-callback method
COPPA plan includes data minimization inventory listing no PII beyond operational necessity
FERPA checklist maps all education record types and permissible disclosure paths
WCAG 2.1 AA conformance statement lists all tested criteria with pass/fail status
Data retention policy specifies retention period and deletion SLA per data category

plan.description: Draft Terms of Service, Privacy Policy (COPPA-compliant with plain-language children's summary), and data processing agreements for third-party AI and analytics providers. Review IP ownership of AI-generated tutoring content.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy policy has a dedicated children's section written at 6th-grade reading level
ToS covers content ownership, AI-generated outputs, and acceptable use policy
DPA template is GDPR and CCPA compatible for school district procurement
IP review confirms no third-party model outputs create ownership conflicts for generated content
FERPA/COPPA compliance if minors involved

plan.description: Produce STRIDE threat model for ai_tutor, define authentication strategy (JWT plus OAuth2, parental consent tokens), data encryption requirements, prompt injection mitigation, and penetration test plan with special focus on child data protection.
acceptance_criteria: STRIDE threat model covers all 6 categories with documented mitigations for all high and critical risks
Auth architecture supports guardian parent role with child sub-accounts and consent tokens
Prompt injection mitigation strategy documented for all Socratic engine student inputs
Pen test plan covers OWASP Top 10 and OWASP LLM Top 10
All child PII encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3) per requirements

plan.description: Design OpenAPI 3.1 spec for all ai_tutor endpoints: auth, student sessions, adaptive learning engine, Socratic chat, multi-subject content, quizzes, progress analytics, parent dashboard, and admin routes.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAPI spec validates with zero errors via swagger-parser
All endpoints have request and response schemas with typed examples
Error catalog covers all 4xx and 5xx cases with human-readable messages
Parental consent flow fully specified including email verification callback
Rate limiting headers documented per endpoint group

plan.description: Design PostgreSQL schema for ai_tutor: users, guardian_relationships, subjects, curriculum_nodes (knowledge graph with prerequisites), learning_sessions, quiz_attempts, per-student adaptive_state, progress_snapshots, and FERPA-compliant append-only audit_events table.

payload.compliance_note: audit_events is append-only; UPDATE and DELETE revoked at DB level

acceptance_criteria: ERD covers all entities with labeled relationships and cardinalities
Schema includes soft-delete and created_at/updated_at timestamps on all mutable tables
audit_events table has UPDATE and DELETE privileges revoked for all application roles
Adaptive state schema supports per-student per-subject mastery probability vectors
Alembic migrations run idempotently on clean and existing databases
Seed data populates 3 subjects with at least 20 curriculum nodes each

plan.description: Design and implement the adaptive learning model pipeline: Deep Knowledge Tracing (DKT) for student mastery estimation, Item Response Theory (IRT) for difficulty calibration, and learning path recommendation. Train on synthetic interactions, export as REST inference service.
acceptance_criteria: DKT model achieves AUC >= 0.82 on held-out 20% test split
IRT calibration produces difficulty and discrimination parameters for all curriculum nodes
Inference service latency p99 <= 100ms for next-node recommendation
Model card documents training data, evaluation metrics, known limitations, and bias analysis
Serve endpoint exposes REST API compatible with backend integration spec from step 8
Bias evaluation performed
Model card generated

plan.description: Build analytics data pipeline: xAPI-compatible clickstream event schema, ingestion layer, nightly dbt aggregation jobs for learning KPIs, and materialized views for parent dashboard and admin analytics.
acceptance_criteria: xAPI event schema covers 8 verb types: answered, completed, passed, failed, progressed, experienced, launched, exited
dbt models produce daily, weekly, and monthly aggregations with no nulls on key metric fields
Pipeline sustains 10k events per minute without backpressure under load test
Parent dashboard materialized view returns in <= 200ms for 90-day lookback window
All PII fields hashed or pseudonymized in analytics aggregation tables

plan.description: Scaffold ai_tutor SAGE solution: write project.yaml, prompts.yaml, and tasks.yaml. Configure Multi-Agent Coordinator pattern with Socratic Tutor Agent (ReAct), Adaptive Router Agent, and Content Critic Agent. Add safety filters, prompt injection guards, and HITL gates for curriculum edits.
acceptance_criteria: project.yaml defines solution with active_modules list and theme block
prompts.yaml defines socratic_tutor, adaptive_router, and content_critic roles with complete system prompts
tasks.yaml defines task types: socratic_session, quiz_generation, path_adaptation, progress_report
Safety filter rejects responses containing PII, adult content, or detected prompt injection patterns
HITL gates configured for curriculum_node_edit and agent_hire task types
Solution loads without errors via make run PROJECT=ai_tutor

plan.description: Implement Socratic Tutor Agent using ReAct pattern: receives student question or topic, retrieves curriculum node from knowledge graph, generates Socratic follow-up questions (never direct answers), evaluates student response quality, updates mastery state, and selects next pedagogical move. Integrates with adaptive model from step 10.
acceptance_criteria: Agent never reveals direct answers — all outputs are questions, guided hints, or Socratic prompts
ReAct loop completes in <= 3s per turn at p95 latency
Agent correctly writes updated mastery probability to adaptive state after each student response
Socratic question quality scored >= 4/5 by content_critic agent on 20 benchmark test cases
Age-appropriate language verified via Flesch-Kincaid grade matching student age group setting
Prompt injection attempts in student input caught and deflected with safe fallback response

plan.description: Build FastAPI backend for ai_tutor: JWT + OAuth2 authentication with parental consent flow, student and guardian CRUD, learning session management, Socratic chat relay, adaptive engine client, progress aggregation endpoints, parent dashboard API, and admin routes.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints from OpenAPI spec implemented and passing contract tests
Parental consent flow: parent email verified, consent recorded, child account activates atomically
Audit logger writes every student data access event to FERPA audit_events with user, timestamp, and record type
Rate limiting enforced: 60 req/min per student session, 200 req/min per parent account
All endpoints return <= 200ms p95 latency under 100 concurrent users in load test
Guardian-child relationship enforced at middleware layer — parent cannot access another family's data

plan.description: Build React 18 + TypeScript student-facing UI: onboarding wizard (age, grade, subject selection), adaptive Socratic chat interface, quiz component supporting multiple-choice and fill-in-blank, subject progress rings, streak tracker, and achievement badges. WCAG 2.1 AA compliant.
acceptance_criteria: All pages pass axe-core accessibility audit with zero critical violations
Socratic chat interface shows typing indicator, message history, and collapsible hint panel
Progress rings animate on load and update in real-time after session completion
Quiz component renders multiple-choice, fill-in-blank, and drag-to-match question types
Lighthouse scores >= 90 for Performance, Accessibility, and Best Practices
All pages are mobile-responsive and tested at 375px, 768px, and 1280px breakpoints

plan.description: Build parent dashboard UI: child account management, daily and weekly progress summaries, time-spent analytics charts, subject mastery heatmaps, session history log, parental controls (time limits, subject restrictions), and COPPA consent management with revocation flow.
acceptance_criteria: Parent can add, edit, and deactivate child accounts with COPPA consent verification shown at each step
Progress summary shows 7-day and 30-day mastery trends per subject with trend arrows
Parental time limit controls save and are enforced end-to-end (E2E test confirms enforcement)
All charts render in <= 500ms for 90-day data range
COPPA consent withdrawal flow triggers PII deletion and shows completion confirmation within UI

plan.description: Set up Docker Compose for local dev stack (PostgreSQL, Redis, backend, ML inference, frontend), Kubernetes manifests for production (HPA, PodDisruptionBudget, NetworkPolicy for PII isolation), and GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with staging auto-deploy and prod manual gate.

payload.security: NetworkPolicy isolates ML inference and PII services from internet egress

acceptance_criteria: docker-compose up --build starts full stack in <= 3 minutes on a clean machine
CI pipeline runs lint, type-check, unit tests, and integration tests on every PR
CD pipeline auto-deploys to staging on main merge; prod deploy requires manual approval gate
K8s HPA scales backend pods from 2 to 20 replicas based on CPU and memory thresholds
NetworkPolicy prevents ML inference service from initiating external internet connections
All secrets injected via Kubernetes Secrets or external Vault — zero plaintext credentials in manifests

plan.description: Write unit tests (pytest for backend, Vitest for frontend), integration tests (database and API contract), and Playwright E2E tests covering: student onboarding, full Socratic session loop, adaptive path update, parent dashboard review, and cross-family data isolation.
acceptance_criteria: Backend unit test line coverage >= 80% across all service modules
Frontend component tests cover all interactive states: loading, error, success, and empty
E2E test covers full student journey: login to topic select to Socratic chat to quiz to progress update
E2E FERPA isolation test confirms parent cannot retrieve another family's data across 50 attempts
Full test suite completes in CI in <= 10 minutes
All tests pass 3 consecutive runs with no flakiness

plan.description: Design comprehensive QA test plan covering functional, regression, performance, accessibility, and child-safety categories. Define edge cases for adaptive engine boundary conditions, Socratic agent safety boundaries, and COPPA/FERPA compliance verification scenarios.

payload.load_targets: 500 concurrent student sessions, 100 concurrent parent sessions sustained 10 minutes

acceptance_criteria: Test plan covers all 5 core features with positive, negative, and edge case scenarios
Child safety scenarios include 20 adversarial student inputs testing jailbreak and inappropriate topic attempts
Performance test plan defines ramp-up profile and pass/fail thresholds per SLA
Accessibility test cases map to each WCAG 2.1 AA criterion in scope
Compliance scenarios verify: COPPA deletion within 72h, FERPA access log completeness, and consent revocation end-to-end

plan.description: Execute system-level integration testing: 20-student adaptive learning simulation over 50 sessions each, Socratic agent child-safety adversarial review, parent dashboard data accuracy validation, and load test at 500 concurrent users for 10 minutes.

payload.pass_thresholds: mastery delta >= 15% over 50 sessions, API p99 <= 500ms at 500 CU, zero cross-family data leakage

acceptance_criteria: 20-student simulation shows average mastery probability delta >= 15% after 50 sessions per subject
Socratic agent achieves >= 90% safe response rate on child-safety adversarial input set
Parent dashboard data matches source-of-truth database within 5-minute reporting lag
p99 API latency <= 500ms sustained over 10 minutes at 500 concurrent users
Data isolation penetration test: zero cross-family data leakage detected across 100 test attempts

plan.description: Produce final COPPA evidence package (consent audit trail, data inventory, deletion verification report) and FERPA evidence package (education records inventory, disclosure log schema, access control matrix). Run and publish WCAG 2.1 AA conformance report.
acceptance_criteria: COPPA consent audit trail captures: parent email, timestamp, IP, consent version, child account ID per event
Data inventory classifies all PII fields with collection purpose, storage location, and retention period
Deletion verification report confirms automated PII removal within 72h SLA with timestamped proof
FERPA access control matrix maps all roles to permissible record types with deny-by-default shown
WCAG 2.1 AA conformance report produced by axe-core automated scan plus manual review with results for all applicable criteria

plan.description: Set up i18n infrastructure (react-i18next + i18next-backend, backend locale middleware), extract all UI strings to translation files, and deliver initial translations for en-US, es, and fr. Configure locale-aware formatting for dates, numbers, grade levels, and subject names.
acceptance_criteria: All UI text extracted to translation JSON files with zero hardcoded strings remaining in components
Spanish and French translations complete and reviewed for all student-facing and parent-facing pages
Language switcher toggles locale without full page reload
Date and number formatting verified correct per locale via unit tests
RTL layout scaffolded and layout does not break at 0% translation coverage for Arabic locale

plan.description: Develop firmware for ai_tutor companion learning device (ARM Cortex-M4 offline tablet variant): FreeRTOS tasks for BLE session sync, e-ink display driver for offline lesson cards, offline quiz cache manager, and low-power sleep/wake on proximity sensor trigger.
acceptance_criteria: FreeRTOS boots and schedules 4 tasks (BLE sync, display, cache, power manager) without deadlock over 24h run
BLE sync task transfers quiz cache from backend within 30s of proximity detection event
e-ink driver renders a full lesson card frame in <= 500ms
Quiz cache stores >= 50 offline quiz items in FLASH with CRC integrity check
Deep sleep current draw measured <= 5uA on target hardware

plan.description: Build SystemC behavioral simulation of companion device SoC: ARM Cortex-M4 core model, BLE transceiver simulation, e-ink display controller model, and power consumption testbench to validate sleep/wake power budget before PCB fabrication.
acceptance_criteria: SoC simulation boots firmware image and completes BLE sync scenario without segfault or assertion failure
Power testbench reports <= 5uA simulated sleep current consistent with firmware specification
e-ink controller model produces pixel-accurate frame buffer output for reference test image
All 4 testbench scenarios complete with zero assertion failures
Full simulation run completes in <= 60s wall-clock time for 10s simulated time

plan.description: Capture KiCad schematic for ai_tutor companion device: STM32L4R5 MCU, nRF52840 BLE SoC, e-ink FPC connector, USB-C PD charging (BQ25895), LiPo management, VCNL4040 proximity sensor. Generate fabrication-ready BOM with RoHS and REACH compliance confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: Schematic passes ERC with zero errors
PCB layout passes DRC with zero violations against JLCPCB 4-layer design rules
BOM includes manufacturer part numbers and lead-free (RoHS) certification for all components
No REACH-restricted substances flagged in BOM material declaration
Gerber files exported and validated as fabrication-ready by DFM check

plan.description: Write firmware unit tests (Unity framework) and HIL test specification for ai_tutor companion device: BLE sync correctness, offline quiz cache CRC integrity, e-ink display driver pixel accuracy, and power mode transition stability over 1000 sleep/wake cycles.
acceptance_criteria: Unity test suite achieves >= 85% firmware line coverage across all modules
BLE sync test verifies correct quiz data transfer for 10 distinct test payloads
Quiz cache test confirms read/write integrity for 100-item cache with per-item CRC validation
HIL power cycle test completes 1000 sleep/wake cycles with zero crashes or data corruption events
All Unity tests pass on both target hardware (STM32L4R5) and SystemC simulation

plan.description: Produce user guides for students (K-8 and 9-12 variants), parents, and school administrators. Write in-app onboarding wizard copy and tooltip content. Create educator quick-start guide for school deployment. Produce 3-minute product walkthrough video script.
acceptance_criteria: K-8 student guide passes Flesch-Kincaid readability check at grade 4-6 level
Parent guide covers account setup, privacy controls, and progress chart interpretation in <= 10 pages
Admin guide covers school deployment, SSO configuration, and FERPA data request workflow
Video script reviewed and approved by product_manager for tone and accuracy
All in-app tooltip copy reviewed for COPPA-compliant language with no data collection incentives

plan.description: Configure production observability: Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboards (API health, ML inference throughput, adaptive model accuracy drift, child-safety filter trigger rate, user acquisition funnel), PagerDuty alerting, and SRE runbooks for all critical failure modes.

payload.sla_targets: 99.9% monthly uptime, p99 API <= 500ms, safety filter response <= 50ms

acceptance_criteria: All 5 Grafana dashboards deployed and showing live production data
PagerDuty alert fires within 2 minutes when API error rate exceeds 1% over a 5-minute window
Adaptive model accuracy drift alert triggers when DKT AUC degrades >= 5% from deployment baseline
Safety filter trigger rate dashboard shows per-age-group anomaly detection with drill-down
SRE runbooks cover: database failover, ML service restart, COPPA deletion SLA breach, and Socratic agent degraded mode

flaws: Hardware simulation (step 24) depends on firmware (step 23), but this is backwards — you simulate the SoC model first and develop firmware against it. Writing firmware before the SystemC model means the simulation validates nothing; it just mirrors assumptions already baked into the firmware.

ML model (step 10) trains on 'synthetic_500k_student_interactions' — synthetic data cannot produce a valid IRT calibration. IRT discrimination and difficulty parameters require real item response distributions. A DKT trained on synthetic data will exhibit severe distribution shift on real students. AUC >= 0.82 on a synthetic test split is a meaningless benchmark.

Step 13 acceptance criteria grades Socratic question quality via content_critic agent ('scored >= 4/5 by content_critic on 20 benchmark cases') — this is circular AI-grading-AI validation with no human expert review. A language model scoring another language model's pedagogical quality does not constitute educational validation.

No curriculum content strategy exists anywhere in 28 steps. Step 9 seeds '20 curriculum nodes per subject' but no step specifies who authors the educational content, how it is reviewed for accuracy, or how it is maintained. The Socratic tutor (step 13) cannot function without high-quality knowledge graph content.

COPPA and FERPA legal documents in step 6 are drafted by an 'agent_role: legal_advisor' — AI-generated legal documents for a platform collecting data from children under 13 are not legally defensible. No external attorney review step exists. FTC enforcement actions against COPPA violators have resulted in multi-million dollar fines.

Step 7 produces a penetration test plan but no step executes it. For a regulated platform handling child PII, a plan with no execution is compliance theater. The actual pentest must be conducted by qualified third parties before launch.

Performance targets are internally inconsistent: step 14 requires p95 <= 200ms under 100 concurrent users; step 20 requires p99 <= 500ms at 500 concurrent users. These are different percentiles at different load levels. The load test does not validate the development acceptance criterion.

No payment processing implementation step exists despite step 1 defining B2C and B2B2C pricing tiers and step 6 including a DPA for the payment processor. A subscription product with no billing implementation is unlaunchable.

No email infrastructure step. COPPA verifiable parental consent requires email-plus-callback (step 5 specifies this). No SES/SendGrid/Postmark configuration step exists. The parental consent flow in step 14 cannot function without a deployed transactional email service.

Step 22 localization accepts 'RTL layout scaffolded and layout does not break at 0% translation coverage for Arabic locale' — this criterion validates nothing. 0% translation coverage is not a shipped locale. This defers real RTL work while claiming it as done.

No CDN or media/asset storage step. An educational platform for K-12 with images, diagrams, math notation rendering, and potentially audio requires object storage and CDN configuration. This is absent from both step 17 (infra) and all frontend steps.

Step 9 states 'Alembic migrations run idempotently on clean and existing databases' — Alembic migrations are sequential, not idempotent by design. Adding a non-null column without a server default will fail on existing populated databases. This acceptance criterion will fail in staging the first time a data migration is attempted.

No database high-availability configuration. Step 17 defines K8s with HPA but no PostgreSQL primary-replica failover, no backup schedule, no point-in-time recovery. A single PostgreSQL instance holding student PII and FERPA audit records has no DR posture.

Step 28 defines a DKT accuracy drift alert but no remediation path. When the alert fires (AUC degrades 5%), there is no retraining pipeline, no model versioning, no rollback procedure. The alert tells you the model is broken with no plan to fix it.

No GDPR step. The DPA template in step 6 is described as 'GDPR and CCPA compatible' but GDPR compliance for EU students is never explicitly addressed. If the product launches to any EU users, data residency, lawful basis for processing minors' data, and DPO designation are all required.
suggestions: Swap steps 23 and 24: design and validate the SystemC SoC simulation first, then develop firmware against the validated hardware model. This is standard embedded development practice.

Replace synthetic DKT training with a hybrid approach: use a pre-trained open educational DKT checkpoint (e.g., from ASSISTments dataset) as baseline, then plan online learning updates from real user interactions post-launch. Document this limitation explicitly in the model card.

Add a step between steps 9 and 10: 'CONTENT_CREATION — curriculum authors (SMEs) produce and review knowledge graph content for 3 subjects, minimum 50 nodes each, validated by a licensed educator.' The ML model and Socratic agent depend on this.

Add a step for external legal review: 'LEGAL_REVIEW — licensed COPPA/FERPA attorney reviews privacy policy, ToS, and parental consent flow before launch.' This should gate step 21 (compliance evidence package).

Add an actual pentest execution step after step 20: 'PENTEST_EXECUTION — third-party security firm conducts black-box pen test against staging environment, OWASP Top 10 + OWASP LLM Top 10, results remediated before prod deploy.'
Add a step for payment implementation: 'BILLING — Stripe subscription integration, B2C and B2B2C pricing tiers, invoice generation, school district PO flow, refund handling.'
Add email infrastructure to step 17 or as a separate step: configure transactional email service (SES + custom domain), DKIM/SPF/DMARC, unsubscribe compliance, and delivery monitoring.

Replace content_critic AI grading of Socratic quality with a human educator review panel: define a rubric validated by a credentialed educator, have 3 reviewers score 50 benchmark cases, establish inter-rater reliability before using the AI critic as a proxy.
Unify latency targets: pick one percentile (p99) and one load level (500 CU) and ensure step 14 acceptance criteria reference the same SLA as step 20 load test.
Add PostgreSQL HA to step 17: Patroni or RDS Multi-AZ, automated backups with tested restore, and a documented RTO/RPO target.

Step 10 should include a production feedback loop design: how does new student interaction data get incorporated into model updates? Define retraining cadence, shadow mode evaluation, and rollback criteria before deployment.
Add GDPR as an explicit compliance scope in step 5, with data residency decision (EU region or adequacy decision), lawful basis for processing, and DPO designation if required by volume.
missing: Curriculum content authoring and SME review — no human educator creates or validates the educational content that the entire product depends on
Payment/billing implementation — no Stripe or equivalent integration despite pricing tiers in the PRD
Transactional email infrastructure — required for COPPA parental consent but absent from all steps
CDN and object storage — required for images, math notation, audio, and diagrams in educational content
External attorney review of COPPA/FERPA legal documents — AI-drafted compliance docs for children's data are not legally defensible
Actual penetration test execution — a pentest plan with no execution provides no security assurance
Model retraining pipeline — the adaptive ML model has no mechanism for improvement post-deployment despite drift alerting in step 28
GDPR compliance — EU student data processing, lawful basis, data residency, DPO designation
Database disaster recovery — backup schedule, point-in-time recovery, failover testing
Pedagogical validation by credentialed educators — no learning scientist reviews the Socratic approach, turn limits, or mastery thresholds
IRB or equivalent ethical review for collecting behavioral data from minors
School SSO integration (SAML/OIDC with district IdP) — mentioned in v2 features but required for B2B2C school deals at any meaningful scale
Content moderation for student-generated text — the Socratic chat receives free-form student input that needs moderation beyond prompt injection detection
security_risks: AI-generated COPPA legal documents: if the parental consent mechanism or privacy policy has gaps, FTC enforcement exposure is direct. Children's data requires verified legal review, not LLM drafting.

Prompt injection via student input (step 13) relies on 'prompt_injection_guard' defined in YAML config with no implementation step. Guard effectiveness is untested against real adversarial inputs from school-age children who have demonstrated creativity in jailbreaking educational AI tools.

Guardian-child relationship enforced 'at middleware layer' (step 14) — if this enforcement is in middleware and not also enforced at the database query level with row-level security, a middleware bypass or query parameter injection could expose cross-family data. Defense-in-depth requires both layers.

JWT token handling for parental consent tokens (step 7) — no token revocation mechanism specified. If a parent's email is compromised after consent is granted, there is no path to invalidate the consent token short of account deletion.

ML inference service exposed as REST API (step 10) — if the inference endpoint is reachable without authentication, an attacker can probe the adaptive model to enumerate student mastery states or manipulate difficulty calibration by poisoning the inference inputs. No auth requirement on the ML service endpoint is specified.

Step 17 NetworkPolicy 'prevents ML inference service from initiating external internet connections' but does not address inbound — the ML service must not be directly reachable from the internet. The policy direction (egress-only) is insufficient; ingress restriction from outside the cluster is also required.

FERPA audit_events append-only enforcement relies on 'UPDATE and DELETE privileges revoked at DB level' (step 9) — this is bypassed by a superuser or DBA with ALTER TABLE privileges. True append-only compliance requires immutable storage (WAL-based or external audit log service) that survives even a superuser compromise.

No secret rotation plan — step 17 requires secrets injected via K8s Secrets or Vault, but no rotation schedule or automated rotation mechanism is defined. Static LLM API keys, database passwords, and JWT signing keys that never rotate are a long-term compromise vector.

Hardware companion device (steps 23-25) has no secure boot, firmware signing, or OTA update authentication specified. An unsigned firmware OTA on a children's device is a critical supply chain attack vector.
final_review.summary: This is an extraordinarily ambitious plan that conflates an AI tutoring SaaS, a regulated children's data platform, and a custom hardware device into a single build. The planning discipline is high — dependency ordering is mostly correct, acceptance criteria are specific, and compliance awareness (COPPA/FERPA) is present from the start. However, the plan has several category-level failures that block production readiness. The most critical: there is no curriculum content step, meaning the Socratic tutor and adaptive model have nothing to teach; the ML model trains on synthetic data that cannot produce valid IRT calibration; legal documents are AI-drafted with no attorney review for a product handling children's PII; and a penetration test plan exists without execution. The hardware track (steps 23-25) has its dependency ordering inverted and adds scope that has no place in a software product MVP. For an MVP of just the web-based tutoring product (steps 1-22 minus hardware), removing the synthetic-only ML training path and adding the five missing infrastructure pieces (email, billing, CDN, content authoring, external legal review), this plan could reach 68-72. As written, targeting a production launch with children's data and regulatory exposure, it scores 54 — too many fundamental gaps to ship safely.

flaws: Hardware simulation (step 24) depends on firmware (step 23), but this is backwards — you simulate the SoC model first and develop firmware against it. Writing firmware before the SystemC model means the simulation validates nothing; it just mirrors assumptions already baked into the firmware.

ML model (step 10) trains on 'synthetic_500k_student_interactions' — synthetic data cannot produce a valid IRT calibration. IRT discrimination and difficulty parameters require real item response distributions. A DKT trained on synthetic data will exhibit severe distribution shift on real students. AUC >= 0.82 on a synthetic test split is a meaningless benchmark.

Step 13 acceptance criteria grades Socratic question quality via content_critic agent ('scored >= 4/5 by content_critic on 20 benchmark cases') — this is circular AI-grading-AI validation with no human expert review. A language model scoring another language model's pedagogical quality does not constitute educational validation.

No curriculum content strategy exists anywhere in 28 steps. Step 9 seeds '20 curriculum nodes per subject' but no step specifies who authors the educational content, how it is reviewed for accuracy, or how it is maintained. The Socratic tutor (step 13) cannot function without high-quality knowledge graph content.

COPPA and FERPA legal documents in step 6 are drafted by an 'agent_role: legal_advisor' — AI-generated legal documents for a platform collecting data from children under 13 are not legally defensible. No external attorney review step exists. FTC enforcement actions against COPPA violators have resulted in multi-million dollar fines.

Step 7 produces a penetration test plan but no step executes it. For a regulated platform handling child PII, a plan with no execution is compliance theater. The actual pentest must be conducted by qualified third parties before launch.

Performance targets are internally inconsistent: step 14 requires p95 <= 200ms under 100 concurrent users; step 20 requires p99 <= 500ms at 500 concurrent users. These are different percentiles at different load levels. The load test does not validate the development acceptance criterion.

No payment processing implementation step exists despite step 1 defining B2C and B2B2C pricing tiers and step 6 including a DPA for the payment processor. A subscription product with no billing implementation is unlaunchable.

No email infrastructure step. COPPA verifiable parental consent requires email-plus-callback (step 5 specifies this). No SES/SendGrid/Postmark configuration step exists. The parental consent flow in step 14 cannot function without a deployed transactional email service.

Step 22 localization accepts 'RTL layout scaffolded and layout does not break at 0% translation coverage for Arabic locale' — this criterion validates nothing. 0% translation coverage is not a shipped locale. This defers real RTL work while claiming it as done.

No CDN or media/asset storage step. An educational platform for K-12 with images, diagrams, math notation rendering, and potentially audio requires object storage and CDN configuration. This is absent from both step 17 (infra) and all frontend steps.

Step 9 states 'Alembic migrations run idempotently on clean and existing databases' — Alembic migrations are sequential, not idempotent by design. Adding a non-null column without a server default will fail on existing populated databases. This acceptance criterion will fail in staging the first time a data migration is attempted.

No database high-availability configuration. Step 17 defines K8s with HPA but no PostgreSQL primary-replica failover, no backup schedule, no point-in-time recovery. A single PostgreSQL instance holding student PII and FERPA audit records has no DR posture.

Step 28 defines a DKT accuracy drift alert but no remediation path. When the alert fires (AUC degrades 5%), there is no retraining pipeline, no model versioning, no rollback procedure. The alert tells you the model is broken with no plan to fix it.

No GDPR step. The DPA template in step 6 is described as 'GDPR and CCPA compatible' but GDPR compliance for EU students is never explicitly addressed. If the product launches to any EU users, data residency, lawful basis for processing minors' data, and DPO designation are all required.
suggestions: Swap steps 23 and 24: design and validate the SystemC SoC simulation first, then develop firmware against the validated hardware model. This is standard embedded development practice.

Replace synthetic DKT training with a hybrid approach: use a pre-trained open educational DKT checkpoint (e.g., from ASSISTments dataset) as baseline, then plan online learning updates from real user interactions post-launch. Document this limitation explicitly in the model card.

Add a step between steps 9 and 10: 'CONTENT_CREATION — curriculum authors (SMEs) produce and review knowledge graph content for 3 subjects, minimum 50 nodes each, validated by a licensed educator.' The ML model and Socratic agent depend on this.

Add a step for external legal review: 'LEGAL_REVIEW — licensed COPPA/FERPA attorney reviews privacy policy, ToS, and parental consent flow before launch.' This should gate step 21 (compliance evidence package).

Add an actual pentest execution step after step 20: 'PENTEST_EXECUTION — third-party security firm conducts black-box pen test against staging environment, OWASP Top 10 + OWASP LLM Top 10, results remediated before prod deploy.'
Add a step for payment implementation: 'BILLING — Stripe subscription integration, B2C and B2B2C pricing tiers, invoice generation, school district PO flow, refund handling.'
Add email infrastructure to step 17 or as a separate step: configure transactional email service (SES + custom domain), DKIM/SPF/DMARC, unsubscribe compliance, and delivery monitoring.

Replace content_critic AI grading of Socratic quality with a human educator review panel: define a rubric validated by a credentialed educator, have 3 reviewers score 50 benchmark cases, establish inter-rater reliability before using the AI critic as a proxy.
Unify latency targets: pick one percentile (p99) and one load level (500 CU) and ensure step 14 acceptance criteria reference the same SLA as step 20 load test.
Add PostgreSQL HA to step 17: Patroni or RDS Multi-AZ, automated backups with tested restore, and a documented RTO/RPO target.

Step 10 should include a production feedback loop design: how does new student interaction data get incorporated into model updates? Define retraining cadence, shadow mode evaluation, and rollback criteria before deployment.
Add GDPR as an explicit compliance scope in step 5, with data residency decision (EU region or adequacy decision), lawful basis for processing, and DPO designation if required by volume.
missing: Curriculum content authoring and SME review — no human educator creates or validates the educational content that the entire product depends on
Payment/billing implementation — no Stripe or equivalent integration despite pricing tiers in the PRD
Transactional email infrastructure — required for COPPA parental consent but absent from all steps
CDN and object storage — required for images, math notation, audio, and diagrams in educational content
External attorney review of COPPA/FERPA legal documents — AI-drafted compliance docs for children's data are not legally defensible
Actual penetration test execution — a pentest plan with no execution provides no security assurance
Model retraining pipeline — the adaptive ML model has no mechanism for improvement post-deployment despite drift alerting in step 28
GDPR compliance — EU student data processing, lawful basis, data residency, DPO designation
Database disaster recovery — backup schedule, point-in-time recovery, failover testing
Pedagogical validation by credentialed educators — no learning scientist reviews the Socratic approach, turn limits, or mastery thresholds
IRB or equivalent ethical review for collecting behavioral data from minors
School SSO integration (SAML/OIDC with district IdP) — mentioned in v2 features but required for B2B2C school deals at any meaningful scale
Content moderation for student-generated text — the Socratic chat receives free-form student input that needs moderation beyond prompt injection detection
security_risks: AI-generated COPPA legal documents: if the parental consent mechanism or privacy policy has gaps, FTC enforcement exposure is direct. Children's data requires verified legal review, not LLM drafting.

Prompt injection via student input (step 13) relies on 'prompt_injection_guard' defined in YAML config with no implementation step. Guard effectiveness is untested against real adversarial inputs from school-age children who have demonstrated creativity in jailbreaking educational AI tools.

Guardian-child relationship enforced 'at middleware layer' (step 14) — if this enforcement is in middleware and not also enforced at the database query level with row-level security, a middleware bypass or query parameter injection could expose cross-family data. Defense-in-depth requires both layers.

JWT token handling for parental consent tokens (step 7) — no token revocation mechanism specified. If a parent's email is compromised after consent is granted, there is no path to invalidate the consent token short of account deletion.

ML inference service exposed as REST API (step 10) — if the inference endpoint is reachable without authentication, an attacker can probe the adaptive model to enumerate student mastery states or manipulate difficulty calibration by poisoning the inference inputs. No auth requirement on the ML service endpoint is specified.

Step 17 NetworkPolicy 'prevents ML inference service from initiating external internet connections' but does not address inbound — the ML service must not be directly reachable from the internet. The policy direction (egress-only) is insufficient; ingress restriction from outside the cluster is also required.

FERPA audit_events append-only enforcement relies on 'UPDATE and DELETE privileges revoked at DB level' (step 9) — this is bypassed by a superuser or DBA with ALTER TABLE privileges. True append-only compliance requires immutable storage (WAL-based or external audit log service) that survives even a superuser compromise.

No secret rotation plan — step 17 requires secrets injected via K8s Secrets or Vault, but no rotation schedule or automated rotation mechanism is defined. Static LLM API keys, database passwords, and JWT signing keys that never rotate are a long-term compromise vector.

Hardware companion device (steps 23-25) has no secure boot, firmware signing, or OTA update authentication specified. An unsigned firmware OTA on a children's device is a critical supply chain attack vector.
history.summary: This is an extraordinarily ambitious plan that conflates an AI tutoring SaaS, a regulated children's data platform, and a custom hardware device into a single build. The planning discipline is high — dependency ordering is mostly correct, acceptance criteria are specific, and compliance awareness (COPPA/FERPA) is present from the start. However, the plan has several category-level failures that block production readiness. The most critical: there is no curriculum content step, meaning the Socratic tutor and adaptive model have nothing to teach; the ML model trains on synthetic data that cannot produce valid IRT calibration; legal documents are AI-drafted with no attorney review for a product handling children's PII; and a penetration test plan exists without execution. The hardware track (steps 23-25) has its dependency ordering inverted and adds scope that has no place in a software product MVP. For an MVP of just the web-based tutoring product (steps 1-22 minus hardware), removing the synthetic-only ML training path and adding the five missing infrastructure pieces (email, billing, CDN, content authoring, external legal review), this plan could reach 68-72. As written, targeting a production launch with children's data and regulatory exposure, it scores 54 — too many fundamental gaps to ship safely.
